title: US Stock Market: Money market funds shorten maturities as Fed rate uncertainty drives cautious positioning
description: Money market funds are shortening portfolio maturities due to Federal Reserve rate path uncertainty. Managers are favoring floating-rate notes and repurchase agreements for flexibility. This defensive shift occurs as fund assets reach record highs near $8 trillion. They balance limited overnight returns with duration risk from longer maturities. Interest rate futures suggest a potential Fed rate hike in late 2026.

title: BOK hikes rates for first time in 3-1/2 years, signals more
description: South Korea's central bank has increased its benchmark interest rate for the first time in three years.  This move aims to stabilize the currency and counter rising inflationary pressures.  The bank anticipates further tightening of monetary policy in the coming months.  Economic growth is expected to significantly exceed previous forecasts this year.  Analysts predict at least one more rate hike before the year concludes.

title: Dollar Falls and Gold Rallies as US CPI Trails Estimates
description: The dollar index (DXY00 ) is down by -0.55% today. The dollar is retreating today after US June consumer prices rose less than expected, a dovish factor for Fed policy. Also, the benign CPI report has reduced the chances of a Fed rate hike at the FOMC meeting later this...

title: Watch Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh testify live to House Financial Services committee
description: In remarks prepared for the appearance, Warsh promised a vigilant fight to return inflation to the Fed's 2% target.

title: Dollar Slips as Favorable PPI Reduces Fed Rate Hike Chances
description: The dollar index (DXY00 ) is down by -0.12% today. The dollar is under pressure today after US June producer prices rose less than expected, a dovish factor for Fed policy. Also, the benign CPI report has reduced the chances of a Fed rate hike at the FOMC meeting later...

title: US Wholesale Inflation Falls, but Governments Are Still Broke

title: Warsh's Fed plan means it's time to read the bond market backward, says Morgan Stanley chief
description: Ask economists what the most important U.S. asset class is, and they’ll likely tell you bonds. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has said the same. Across Wall...

title: Ex-Fed advisor gets over three years in prison for lying about China ties
description: The case marks one of the most prominent U.S. prosecutions alleging Chinese intelligence targeting of U.S. institutions, as Trump intensifies its pursuit of foreign economic espionage.

title: Fed officials mishandled sensitive information: watchdog report
description: The central bank failed to prevent critical economic information from falling into the hands of "foreign adversaries," according to a report from its inspector general.

title: Despite Falling Oil Prices, There's Now a 73% Chance of an Interest Rate Hike by September -- Here Are the 2 Culprits to Blame
description: There's more to U.S. inflation than just energy prices.

title: A case for less talk? Making sense of Fed's flip-flops over past one year
description: The call from Federal Reserve chief Kevin Warsh to curtail policy guidance is gathering momentum, with Fed Governor Chris Waller making a notable leap from dovish to hawkish stances. Michelle Bowman has also shown a marked transformation in her policy views. Meanwhile, Stephen Miran is advocating for a focus on money supply targeting following his departure from the Fed.

title: Former RBI governor Raghuram Rajan joins US Fed review: What will his role be?
description: Former RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan joins a US Federal Reserve task force to review its balance sheet policy as part of a wider overhaul of monetary policymaking. The panel will assess the Fed's asset holdings and their role in managing inflation and the economy.

title: NY Fed's John Williams: "Unquestionably too-high" inflation may soon subside
description: John Williams, the president of the New York Fed, asserts that inflation is likely at its peak and should soon begin to taper off. He projects that by the end of the year, overall inflation will settle at 3.25%. Furthermore, he expects the unemployment rate to drop to 4% by 2028, attributing this optimism to recent declines in energy prices. The Federal Reserve's latest meeting saw maintained policy rates.

title: Can banks increase FD interest rates as inflation crosses RBI’s target of 4%?
description: Rising inflation has crossed the Reserve Bank of India's target, signaling potential repo rate hikes. Higher inflation and increased credit demand suggest banks might raise fixed deposit interest rates soon. Banks monitor deposit growth and credit demand before adjusting their fixed deposit rates. High yields on government securities and small savings schemes also pressure banks to increase FD rates.

title: US data surprise eases rate hike fears, also cheers D-Street
description: Indian equities closed higher, mirroring gains across Asian markets after US inflation data. Oil prices around $85 a barrel limited upside, raising supply disruption concerns. The NSE Nifty rose 26.5 points, while the BSE Sensex gained 130.5 points. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have made investor sentiment cautious recently. Foreign portfolio investors sold shares, while domestic institutional investors bought equities.

title: Govt bonds extend recovery tracking rise in US treasuries
description: The benchmark 6.94% ⁠2036 bond yield fell 3 basis points to 6.7436% by 11:10 am, extending its two-day decline to 5 basis points ‌after hitting a three-week high on Tuesday.

title: South Korean shares slump 7% on renewed selling in chipmakers, c.bank hikes rates
description: South Korean shares experienced a significant decline on Thursday, driven by chipmaker stock sell-offs. The Bank of Korea raised its benchmark interest rate for the first time in over three years. This move aimed to stabilize the nation's weakening currency against the US dollar. Major chipmakers like SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics saw substantial drops in their stock values. Despite recent volatility, the KOSPI index has shown strong performance year-to-date.

title: India bonds extend recovery tracking rise in U.S. Treasuries
description: Indian government bonds saw gains for a second straight session on Thursday. This followed overnight increases in U.S. Treasuries after softer economic data emerged. Softer U.S. inflation data eased concerns about an imminent Federal Reserve rate hike. Lower U.S. yields eased pressure on emerging-market debt, helping Indian bonds recover. State-run lenders and foreign investors actively bought bonds, adding support.

title: Stocks Supported By Favorable Inflation News
description: The S&P 500 Index ($SPX ) (SPY ) today is up +0.23%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI ) (DIA ) is up +0.26%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX ) (QQQ ) is up +0.31%. September E-mini S&P futures (ESU26 ) are up +0.27%, and September E-mini Nasdaq futures...

title: India bonds recover on softer US rate outlook; oil risk caps gains
description: Indian government bonds experienced a positive turnaround on Wednesday, recovering from previous dips as soft inflation data from the US eased rate hike apprehensions. However, higher crude oil prices exerted upward pressure on US yields, which limited market gains. Steady buying from state banks and foreign entities bolstered bonds, though traders continue to navigate inflationary and fiscal uncertainties.

title: Gold falls as Mideast escalation dims hopes of easing inflation
description: Gold prices drop as escalating Middle East tensions raise inflation concerns and oil prices soar, impacting financial markets.

title: Explained: 3 reasons why South Korea's Kospi crashed up to 8% today
description: South Korea's Kospi slumped as much as 8%, briefly triggering a sidecar trading curb, as a Bank of Korea rate hike, a sharp sell-off in AI chip giants Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, and concerns over leveraged single-stock ETFs rattled investor sentiment despite the index's strong 2026 gains.

title: US Dollar Price Forecast: Cooling Inflation Challenges DXY – Are GBP/USD and EUR/USD Ready to Rally?
description: Softer U.S. inflation reshapes Fed expectations as DXY consolidates below $101.22. See the latest outlook for EUR/USD and GBP/USD.

title: Bank of Korea raises rate, signals further tightening
description: South Korea raised its key rate to 2.75% as inflation, housing risks and semiconductor-led growth increased pressure for further tightening.

title: Stock Markets Today: Sensex, Nifty Climb on IT Stocks' Rally
description: Indian benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty, saw early gains, primarily driven by IT stocks, amidst expectations of a less aggressive monetary policy from the US Federal Reserve following softer inflation data. Global market trends and upcoming Q1 results are also influencing investor sentiment. Track Sensex, Nifty on july 16.

title: Waiting For a Trigger, Ready to Slide
description: Przemyslaw K. Radomski, CFA, explains why gold and silver shrugged off a cooling CPI, falling PPI, and collapsing rate-hike odds this week, and why he expects the next move to be down.

title: Why Markets Are Declaring Victory on Inflation Too Soon
description: Global Settlement CEO Ryan Kirkley says June's cooler CPI print bought the Fed time, not an exit, as crypto, AI, and stablecoins reshape financial market structure.

title: Dollar near one-month low as cooling inflation curbs Fed hike bets
description: The U.S. dollar weakened near a one-month low on Thursday. Soft inflation data reinforced expectations of a patient Federal Reserve policy. Escalation in Middle East hostilities added upside risk to inflation outlook. Oil prices remained near one-month highs due to regional energy export threats. The U.S. dollar index hovered near its lowest level since June.

title: US producer prices drop 0.3 per cent from May to June on lower energy prices
description: U.S. wholesale inflation fell from May to June on plunging energy prices, but intensifying hostilities with Iran are clouding the outlook.

title: Bitcoin hovers near $64,600 as inflation cools, geopolitical risks cap gains
description: Bitcoin traded flat near $64,600 on Thursday as easing inflation and rising geopolitical tensions kept investors cautious. The world's largest cryptocurrency slipped 0.42% to $64,560, while Ethereum gained 2.24% to $1,917. Among major altcoins, BNB and XRP edged higher, whereas Solana, Tron, Hyperliquid, Dogecoin and Cardano declined by up to 0.95%.
